subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/economics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF/World Bank estimates, V-Dem, RSF, TI, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, OHCHR, CPJ + Efecto Cocuyo/Armando.info); Venezuelan state media and official statistics EXCLUDED there.

Scored on corroborated facts: the July-2024 election widely regarded as stolen (the opposition broadly seen to have won), intensified repression, a ~7.9M migrant exodus, oil-output collapse, and heavy sanctions. Maduro was captured by US forces in January 2026 - the post-Maduro transition is now the dominant uncertainty (a chaotic Chavista/military holdover vs an opposition opening), treated as a fat tail, not a settled outcome.

Standout drags: a collapsed economy, a hollowed repressive state, a humanitarian-scale exodus, and degraded healthcare/infrastructure - now compounded by an abrupt, unresolved leadership vacuum. Overall outcome distribution: negative with a wide two-sided tail.

economic.notes: A collapsed, defaulted oil-rentier with a hyperinflation legacy and a catastrophic resource-curse output collapse.

fiscal_state.notes: Collapsed and opaque, oil-rentier and defaulted, with limited budget credibility despite any sanctions-relief upside. (IMF; CRS)

monetary_independence.notes: A hyperinflation legacy with informal dollarization-creep and zero central-bank independence. (IMF; FocusEconomics)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The bolivar is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Still extreme (the IMF projects triple-digit-plus for 2026), with monthly rates partly stabilising. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: A broken, tiny, heavily dollarized credit system. (IMF; Americas Quarterly)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: The world's largest oil reserves but output collapsed ~80% from peak under mismanagement and sanctions - a textbook catastrophic resource curse, with a tentative 2026 recovery via foreign licenses. (Americas Quarterly)

institutional.notes: No judicial independence, unusable state data, a hollowed bureaucracy, and severe press repression.

rule_of_law.notes: No judicial independence, with a repressive apparatus intact. (V-Dem; HRW)

statistical_integrity.notes: State data is unusable and opaque, with no published election tallies. (Freedom House)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~160/180, with hundreds of outlets closed, blocked sites, and jailed journalists. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: A stolen-election legitimacy crisis, severe repression, and a humanitarian-scale exodus.

political_stability.notes: The July-2024 election was widely regarded as stolen (the opposition broadly seen to have won); Maduro's capture by US forces in Jan 2026 abruptly ended his rule, leaving an unresolved transition (Chavista/military holdover vs an opposition opening) - a large two-sided fat tail. (CSIS; Al Jazeera; CNBC)

civil_liberties.notes: Crimes-against-humanity findings, hundreds of political prisoners, and enforced disappearances. (OHCHR; Amnesty; Foro Penal)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a destination - around 7.9M have fled (~a quarter of the population), with minimal protection for the few migrants. (UNHCR/IOM)

social_cohesion.notes: A humanitarian crisis, mass exodus, and deep polarisation. (UN OCHA; ACAPS)

demographics.notes: Distorted by the emigration of the young and educated. (IOM)

geopolitical.notes: Isolated and heavily sanctioned, with a territorial dispute escalating.

alliance_reliability.notes: Aligned with Russia, China, Cuba, and Iran and heavily sanctioned - isolated and unreliable. (CFR)

conflict_involvement.notes: The Essequibo/Guyana dispute escalated in 2023-25, but US Caribbean military operations culminating in Maduro's Jan 2026 capture reset the picture - direct US action on Venezuelan soil, with an uncertain successor posture. (CSIS; CNBC; Inter-American Dialogue)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Heavily US-sanctioned with capital controls and only conditional, revocable relief. (OFAC)

physical_practical.notes: A collapsed health system, degraded infrastructure, and high crime.

climate.notes: Andean glacier loss (the last glacier gone 2024) plus collapsed institutional readiness plus flood/landslide exposure. (ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: A collapsed public system with disease resurgence and a doctor exodus. (IOM)

infrastructure.notes: Chronic blackouts, water failures, and a degraded grid. (IOM; ACAPS)

crime_safety.notes: High (though down from a catastrophic peak as crime was partly 'exported' with migration). (independent observatories)

summary: A catastrophically mismanaged oil state: the world's largest reserves but output collapsed ~80% from peak, a defaulted and opaque budget, a hyperinflation legacy with informal dollarization, and a broken banking system. Politically it is authoritarian - the July-2024 election was widely regarded as stolen, with intensified repression, crimes-against-humanity findings, and hundreds of political prisoners - and it has produced a humanitarian-scale exodus of nearly a quarter of its population. Heavily sanctioned and isolated, with a collapsed health system and chronic blackouts, the dominant risk is fat-tail and the outcome distribution negative.
